item.title: SpaceX Stock Set for More Than 50% Jump in Just Three Sessions
item.description: SpaceX shares surged in premarket trading, putting the stock on track to extend a rally following its blockbuster debut last week.

item.title: Dubai Property Frenzy Cools But Sellers Hold the Line on Prices
item.description: Three months into a conflict that has roiled the Middle East, a nuanced picture is emerging on the state of Dubai’s housing market. The total value of transactions is down steeply, though prices have proved more resilient and buyers continue to seek out pockets of opportunity.

item.title: STMicro Eyes $1.5 Billion Convertible Bonds After AI-Fueled Jump
item.description: STMicroelectronics NV is looking to raise $1.5 billion from selling debt that can be exchanged into equity after the chipmaker’s shares tripled in value so far this year.

item.title: Israeli Bank to Sever West Bank Ties, Raising Alarm Over Economy
item.description: One of two Israeli banks giving Palestinians access to financial services plans to end the relationship in September, a move Palestinian officials warn could put further pressure on an economy already in turmoil.

item.title: Philippines Returns to Global Bond Market as Oil Pressure Eases
item.description: The Philippines is tapping the international bond market for the second time this year, seizing the opportunity to fund state spending as borrowing costs eased on optimism over a potential US-Iran agreement.

item.title: Equity Market Still Has a Long To-Do List After Iran Peace Deal
item.description: Pricing out a Middle East war looks like the easy part for equities, which must still confront a potentially hawkish new Federal Reserve chair, Washington’s disruptive intervention in the AI trade, and the biggest wave of stock supply in market history.

item.title: Thames Water Edges Closer to Nationalization With Rescue in Doubt
item.description: Thames Water edged nearer to temporary nationalization after a senior government minister said a rescue deal put forward by creditors wasn’t good enough.

item.title: Qatar Plans to Rapidly Restart LNG Production After Hormuz Opens
item.description: Qatar is planning to rapidly boost liquefied natural gas production once the Strait of Hormuz reopens, aiming to restore most of its export capacity within two months, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Sigma’s Doomed Boots Bid Highlights Australian Outbound M&A Woes
item.description: Australia has long been coveted by investment bankers in the Asia Pacific as a market where big mergers and acquisitions transactions take place, and therefore also where advisers make more money on fees.

item.title: Rathbones Shares Plunge on £60 Million Hit From FCA Review
item.description: Rathbones Group Plc shares plunged after the UK wealth manager said it expects to incur £60 million ($80.4 million) of additional costs over the next two years, as it addresses issues found in a watchdog review into how it treats its customers.

item.title: Germany Rejects UniCredit’s Commerzbank Bid on Last Day
item.description: Germany renewed its staunch opposition to UniCredit SpA’s proposed €39 billion ($45 billion) takeover of Commerzbank AG on the final day of the formal acceptance period.

item.title: Chinese Developers Hit Snags in Tokenized Asset Fundraising
item.description: Two cash-strapped Chinese developers looking to raise funds in the tokenized asset market are running into familiar challenges, as weak credit profiles and regulatory uncertainty complicate their efforts.

item.title: More Central Banks Than Ever Say They Will Buy Gold This Year
item.description: More central banks than ever expect to increase their gold reserves, a sign one of the key forces behind bullion’s record-breaking rally remains intact despite this year’s pullback.

item.title: Aluminum Extends Slump as China Data Adds to Downside Pressure
item.description: Aluminum dropped to its lowest since March after weaker-than-expected economic data from China dragged on metals, while investors assessed lingering questions over the execution of a peace deal between Iran and the US.

item.title: FTSE 100 Set to Extend Fall After Missing Rally
BHP and Rio Tinto See India Driving Steel’s Next Wave of Growth
item.description: BHP Group and Rio Tinto Plc are looking increasingly to India as the next big growth engine for global steelmaking, underscoring how the world’s biggest iron ore suppliers are preparing for a future beyond China.

item.title: Anthropic Curbs Show Need for Sovereign AI, Upstage CEO Says
item.description: The US directive to restrict foreign access to Anthropic PBC’s most advanced technologies underscores the importance of South Korea’s sovereign artificial intelligence efforts, according to the head of a leading domestic AI startup.

item.title: Sify Infinit Is Said to Put $391 Million India IPO on Hold
item.description: Sify Infinit Spaces Ltd. has put its planned 37 billion rupee ($391 million) initial public offering on hold, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a growing list of companies delaying share sales because of India’s weak stock market.

item.title: RBA Holds Key Rate, Willing to Tighten Further If Needed
item.description: Australia’s central bank kept its key interest rate unchanged for the first time this year in response to signs that its trio of hikes are beginning to weigh on the nation’s economy.

item.title: AI Re-Rating Fuels 550% Rally in Hong Kong’s Kingboard Laminates
item.description: Kingboard Laminates Holdings Ltd. has rallied more than sixfold this year as investors bet the Chinese supplier to the printed circuit board industry will emerge as a key beneficiary of the AI buildout.

item.title: The Two Clear Winners Are Iran and Markets
item.description: The deal hasn’t had much impact as yet on central banks and rate decisions.

item.title: Burnham’s UK Industrial Revival Plan Unlikely to Bring Back Jobs
item.description: Andy Burnham, the prime ministerial-hopeful who markets worry may be too far to the Left, has at least one economic idea that should go down well across the political spectrum: reverse deindustrialization.

item.title: Bank of Japan Needs Strong Measures to Prop Up Yen: Strategists
item.description: Strategists expect the yen and government bonds to remain under pressure while Japan’s stocks continue to find support following the Bank of Japan’s decision to raise rates on Tuesday.

item.title: Vietnam Stocks See Biggest Foreign Inflows in Nearly Six Years
item.description: Vietnamese shares saw the biggest daily foreign inflows in nearly six years, in a sign of renewed global investor interest in the Southeast Asian market as the Middle East tensions eased.

item.title: China’s Property Stocks Tumble Back to Pre-2024 Stimulus Levels
item.description: Chinese property stocks have slid back to levels seen before a raft of stimulus measures from the authorities in September 2024 drove a market turnaround, underscoring lingering pessimism over the sector.

item.title: Myanmar Growth Forecast Cut to 2% by World Bank on Fuel Shock
item.description: The World Bank cut its annual growth forecast for Myanmar to 2% from 3%, saying a fuel shock linked to the Middle East conflict has raised costs and intensified strains on the war-torn and isolated Southeast Asian country.

item.title: Yen Trims Gain, JGBs Fall on Concern That BOJ Needs to Do More
item.description: The yen pared gains against the dollar and Japanese bonds fell on speculation the Bank of Japan may need to move more decisively to give support to the currency and stem inflation.

item.title: BOJ Raises Rate to 31-Year High and Hits Normalization Milestone
item.description: The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark interest rate to the highest since 1995 and reached another normalization milestone with plans to stop cutting back its bond purchases, at a policy meeting convened without the governor in attendance.

item.title: Swiss Lean in Favor of EU Deal After Dismissing Population Cap
item.description: Swiss voters are more inclined to back closer ties with the European Union than reject it, a poll showed in the wake of a plebiscite that had threatened to upend the relationship.

item.title: Middle East Oil Markets Weaken on Optimism Over Increased Supply
item.description: Middle Eastern crude markets weakened as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz spurred optimism around an increase in flows from the oil-rich region.
